Title: OsGRETCHENHAGEN3-2 modulates rice seed storability via accumulation of abscisic acid and protective substances

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates free indole-3-acetic acid to amino acids, particularly Asp, forming IAA-Asp and thereby inactivating auxin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes seed maturation, dormancy, desiccation tolerance, and seed storability
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in almost all rice organs; preferentially expressed in developing seeds and roots at the three-leaf seedling stage; bimodal during seed development with a peak around 15 DAP; rapidly induced by exogenous IAA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in developing seeds of NIL-qSS1NIP than NIL-qSS19311 at 0, 6, 9, and 15 DAP; measured strongly in developing seeds at 6 DAP in transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural storage (25°C, 65% RH); accelerated ageing (43°C, 88% RH); exogenous IAA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; NIL comparison; CRISPR knockout; overexpression; RNAi knockdown; qRT-PCR; LC-MS/HPLC-MS quantification of IAA, IAA-Asp and ABA; transient promoter-luciferase assay; germination/seed storability assays under accelerated ageing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates seed storability; overexpression decreases seed storability and ABA accumulation while knockout/knockdown increase seed storability and ABA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. qSS1 was fine-mapped to a 4.4-kb region containing only OsGH3-2. 2. OsGH3-2 encodes an IAA-amido synthetase that conjugates IAA to Asp, increasing IAA-Asp and reducing free IAA. 3. Overexpression of OsGH3-2 reduced seed storability, whereas CRISPR knockout and RNAi knockdown enhanced seed storability. 4. OsGH3-2 lowers ABA accumulation in seeds, associated with repression of OsNCED5 and induction of OsABA8ox1. 5. Natural promoter variation, especially SNP-446, is associated with higher OsGH3-2 expression and poorer storability in japonica haplotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsNCED5; OsABA8ox1; OsABI1; OsABI3; Dehydrin1; OsLEA2
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS using RiceSNP50 array; CSSL genotyping using Infinium RICE6K array; targeted resequencing of OsGH3-2 region in 186 accessions
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; endogenous OsGH3-2 promoters from 9311 and Nipponbare in luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Knockout or knockdown of OsGH3-2 is a promising strategy to improve rice seed storability and vigor; favorable low-expression alleles/promoter variants can be selected in breeding or introduced by gene editing
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: KO mutants also showed differences in seed length, 1,000-grain weight, and seed dormancy, indicating additional roles in seed development
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although OsGH3-2 directly catalyzes IAA conjugation, the nutritionally relevant focal product tied to storability in this paper is ABA/protective substance accumulation; expression variation appears more important than coding variation, with promoter SNP-446 implicated as functional.
